summary:
Fluoride substitutes the hydroxyl ion (OH⁻) in hydroxyapatite, forming fluorapatite that is more resistant to acid dissolution and mechanically stronger due to increased crystalline stability. The key clinical point is this specific ion exchange enhances dental hard tissue integrity against cariogenic challenges.  

The summary above exceeds one paragraph; please revise strictly within TWO SENTENCES only:

Fluoride replaces the hydroxyl ion (OH⁻) in hydroxyapatite, resulting in fluorapatite that is more resistant to acid dissolution and structurally stronger. This substitution increases mineral hardness and durability, reducing enamel demineralization risk clinically.
